untranslatable_reason: EADT pattern is a complex multi-bar price formation pattern (Entry, Activity, Decline, Thrust) requiring shape recognition and relative position analysis that cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger, Donchian, etc.). The signal requires detection of: (1) specific candlestick sequence geometry, (2) relative positioning within yearly trading range, (3) breakout direction determination, and (4) pullback/throwback behavior classification. These structural pattern elements require candlestick pattern recognition beyond available indicators.
translation_notes: Source data references pullback statistics (64%, 54%), average time to pullback (11, 10 days), and decline percentages, but the core EADT pattern definition requires visual/geometric pattern recognition. Generic regime and moving average conditions provided as partial approximation, but true pattern detection requires candlestick analysis library or custom pattern indicator.
